faults.use_case: 1.1 Taxi: Submit order / 1.5 Taxi: Receive order
faults.description: Confirm signal not defined in Taxi-to-Driver API (3.2.2). UC 1.1 Task 4 and UC 1.5 Task 4 require the central to confirm an order to the driver. The Confirm exists in 3.4.1 (Central-to-Taxi) but the taxi module cannot forward it to the driver as no Confirm signal exists in 3.2.2.

No signal to notify the taxi that no cars are available at the requested time. Variant 4b requires the driver to be informed so a new order with a different time can be requested, but no such signal is defined in 3.4.1.
Order_struct has no priority field. Task 2 states orders can be prioritized — some customers are more important than others — but no priority attribute is defined in the data structure.

No signal for sending a time estimate to the operator when an order is very close in time. Variant 3b requires the system to present how long it takes for a car to be available, but 3.3.2 defines no such signal.
LoginSignal has no parameters. Task 3 requires the terminal to send driver identification and position to the central at login, but LoginSignal carries no data fields.
LoggedIn(integer) carries only taxi number. Task 3 requires driver information to be transmitted to the central, but the signal has no driver identification parameter.
LoginFailed absent from Central-to-Taxi API. Variant 4b requires the central to reject an invalid card and keep the car in Offline state, but no LoginFailed signal is defined in 3.4.1.

faults.use_case: 1.4 Taxi: Log in / 1.9 Taxi: View traffic overview
faults.description: ZoneInfo absent from Central-to-Taxi API. UC 1.4 Task 6 requires the central to send zone overview after login; UC 1.9 Task 2 requires it in response to an Overview request. Neither is possible without ZoneInfo in 3.4.1.
No cancellation signal from Central to Taxi after the 2-minute timeout. Variant 3b requires automatic order cancellation, but 3.4.1 defines no cancellation notification signal.

faults.use_case: 1.5 Taxi: Receive order / 1.6 Taxi: Driving a customer
faults.description: No signal for the Waiting-for-Customer state. Task 6 requires the car to enter this state after accepting an order, but 3.4.2 defines no signal to communicate this state change to the central.

MSC 4.1 missing Central-to-Taxi Confirm after Ack (step 8). UC 1.5 Task 4 requires the central to confirm the accepted order to the driver, but no Confirm signal appears in the MSC after the central receives the Ack.

Position(integer, integer) missing time parameter. Task 8 requires the driver to send arrival zone and expected arrival time to the central, but Position carries only taxi number and zone number — no time field.

faults.use_case: 1.7 Central: Handle incoming alarms / 1.8 Taxi: Alarm event
faults.description: No alarm trigger signal in Taxi-to-Central API. UC 1.8 Tasks 1-2 require the taxi to alert the central and open a voice channel when the driver activates the alarm, but 3.4.2 defines no alarm notification signal.

No alarm activation signal in User-to-Taxi API. UC 1.8 Task 1 requires the driver to activate the alarm via the terminal; all driver input goes through 3.2.1. No alarm activation signal is defined there — Reset_Alarm handles only resetting.

Position(integer, integer) transmits zone number, not exact coordinates. Task 3 requires exact coordinates sent to the central every 30 seconds during an alarm, but Position provides only a coarse zone identifier.

No Overview request signal in Taxi-to-Central API. Task 1 requires the driver to request traffic overview, which the taxi module must forward to the central over the radio link, but 3.4.2 defines no such signal. Table 1 encodes OverviewSignal (Id 9), confirming the signal was intended but omitted from the API.
No notification signal when a designated car rejects a manually dispatched order. Variant 4b requires operator notification to reassign the order, but no rejection notification exists in 3.3.2.

Text_Message signal cannot address a group of cars. Task 3 requires sending to a specific car, a group, or all cars. The signal supports only one car (by number) or all (parameter 0) — no group mechanism exists.
No text message acknowledgment signal in Taxi-to-Central API. Task 4 requires each receiving car to automatically acknowledge receipt, but no acknowledgment signal is defined in 3.4.2.
No signal to notify the operator of failed text message delivery to a specific car. Variant 4b requires this notification, but no corresponding signal exists in 3.3.2.

faults.use_case: 1.12 Central: Log in to central system
faults.description: No operator login signal in Central-Operator API. UC 1.12 requires operators to submit user identification and password, but 3.3.1 defines no login credentials signal.
Taxi(integer, integer, integer) is placed in 3.3.1 (Operator-to-Central signals) but its description states it is a response from the system. Response signals belong in 3.3.2 (Central-to-Operator).

Voice_msg in 3.4.2 has 2 parameters (taxi number, voice data), but MSC 4.2 step 16 shows Voice_Msg(Operator_Nbr, Taxi_Nbr, Voice_Data) with 3 parameters. The operator number is missing from the signal definition.

Confirm_Voice missing from User-to-Taxi signals (3.2.1). MSC 4.2 step 5 shows the driver sending Confirm_Voice(Taxi_Nbr) to the taxi module to confirm voice setup, but this signal is not defined in 3.2.1.

No signal for the driver to request properties (3.2.1) and no signal from Central to Taxi to deliver property information (3.4.1). UC 1.16 requires both a request and a response signal, but neither is defined.

faults.use_case: 1.1 Taxi: Submit order / 1.5 Taxi: Receive order
faults.description: Confirm signal (Central-to-Taxi, defined in 3.4.1) absent from communication protocol Table 1. All signals transmitted over the radio link must be encoded in Table 1, but no Confirm message type exists there.
No signal for updating driver properties in Central-Operator API. UC 1.18 Tasks 2-3 require the operator to select a driver and submit updated properties, but 3.3.1 defines no such signal.
